p: The Dynamic MDA tool models a radiation detector being driven past a (possibly hypothetical) point source along a straight line. The detector integrates counts over a short transit window centered on the point of closest approach.

The Calculator tab solves the inverse problem: given any two of {source activity, distance of closest approach, speed}, it finds the third at the user-selected confidence level and false-positives-per-hour budget.
The Search tab slides a fixed window across a passthrough / search-mode spectrum file and flags windows whose ROI excess exceeds the per-trial decision threshold derived from the Calculator settings.

The decision threshold Lc is set so that, with no source present, the expected number of windows incorrectly exceeding it is at most the user-specified false-positives per hour. Reducing the false-positive budget or increasing the stride produces a more conservative threshold (and a larger MDA).
Note: a fixed-geometry detector response function (DRF) cannot be used with this tool, because the dynamic-MDA model fundamentally assumes the source distance varies with time.
